senses.definition: a person whose hair is red or a red-colored animal, or a plant with red tops; also a common name for certain red-headed birds or plants
examples: The farmer noticed a redtop among the herd.
In the garden, the redtop variety of lettuce stood out.

senses.definition: plural of redtop; referring to multiple redtop plants or individuals with red hair
examples: Several redtops were planted along the border.
The magazine featured redtops on the cover.

senses.definition: to re-dub; to replace or add new audio to a recording or film in another language or version
examples: The studio decided to redub the scene for a younger audience.
They will redub the film in French.

senses.definition: having had audio replaced in a new language or version
examples: The movie was redubbed into Spanish.
The characters were redubbed for the international release.

senses.definition: one who redubs; a person who re-dubs audio or voices for media
examples: The redubber faced tight deadlines for the soundtrack.
Industry workers include redubbers for international releases.

senses.definition: the act of re-dubbing audio for a media project
examples: Redubbing helped the film reach new markets.
Time was tight during redubbing sessions.

senses.definition: plural of redub; instances of re-dubbing audio
examples: The redubs improved the accessibility of the film.
Several redubs were produced for different regions.

senses.definition: the act of reducing or a reduction; used in Spanish contexts or as a borrowed term
examples: The term reduccion appears in some Latin-based documents.
A reduccion of costs was requested.

senses.definition: to make smaller or less in size, amount, or degree
examples: Reduce the amount of sugar in the recipe.
We need to reduce costs this quarter.

senses.definition: capable of being reduced or made smaller or simpler
examples: The reduceable steps will simplify the process.
Not all materials are reduceable to a single form.

senses.definition: the quality of being reducible; the ease with which something can be reduced
examples: The reduceableness of the model affected its practicality.
Assess the reduceableness before starting the project.

senses.definition: made smaller or less in size or amount
examples: The reduced price attracted many buyers.
Reduced portions were offered during the sale.

senses.definition: an act of reducing or a reduction; a reward or concession in some contexts
examples: The reducement of penalties surprised the audience.
In some languages, reducement refers to a reduction in duties.

senses.definition: causing reduction; tending to reduce
examples: A reducent effect was observed in the experiment.
This factor is reducent to the overall rate.

senses.definition: a device or person that reduces something, such as a tool that lowers or simplifies
examples: A reducer helped cut the noise in the machine.
The company uses a chemical reducer in the process.

senses.definition: plural of reducer; devices or persons that reduce
examples: The machine has several reducers to optimize output.
There are many different types of reducers in electronics.

senses.definition: he/she/it makes something smaller or less
examples: This medicine reduces fever quickly.
The budget reduces by ten percent.

senses.definition: plural of reducibility; the qualities of being reducible
examples: The reducibilities vary across materials.
Researchers study the reducibilities of different compounds.

senses.definition: the quality of being reducible; the possibility to be simplified or made smaller
examples: The reducibility of the equation makes it easier to solve.
In software, reducibility helps optimize performance.

senses.definition: capable of being reduced or simplified
examples: The problem is reducible to a simple equation.
These notes are reducible into a concise summary.

senses.definition: the quality of being reducible
examples: Assess the reduciableness of the materials.
High reducibleness makes the process efficient.

senses.definition: plural of reducibleness; multiple aspects of being reducible
examples: The reduciablenesses of the method were evaluated.
Different reduciablenesses affect efficiency.

senses.definition: in a way that can be reduced or simplified
examples: The equation can be reducibly solved by combining terms.
The path is reducibly complex.

senses.definition: the act of making something smaller or less; the process of reduction
examples: Reducing emissions is important for the environment.
They are reducing the workforce this quarter.

senses.definition: a substance formed by reduction; a chemical product obtained by reduction
examples: The reduct from the reaction was collected.
This reduct has valuable properties for synthesis.
